Amended Notice Of Motion Regarding Plaintiffs Second Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Defendant Sfusd
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDWARD M. ANAYA, Esq. of Anaya Law (Tel: 415-300-0871) for plaintiff;
Atty. OMEED RAJAEE, Esq. of Bertrand, Fox, Elliot, Osman & Wenzel (Tel: 415-353-0999) for defendant San Francisco Unified School District.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DARLENE ANAYA's Motion Regarding Plaintiff's Second Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Defendant SFUSD.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows. As a general matter, the boilerplate objections are improper and I disregard them. Korea Data Systems Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513. RFA 14 - granted. CCP 2033.010 specifically permits an RFA to ask about matters in controversy, and an RFA is an appropriate tool to evaluate what affirmative defenses are being asserted. Sp. Rog. 34 - granted. CCP 2030.220 requires that if an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it must be answered to the extent possible. It is not credible that no one in the District is able to recall any information that may permit a limited answer. RFP 28 - granted. The District's limitation to meeting "with and regarding Plaintiff" is sufficient but the materials must be produced. RFP 30-32 - granted. Considering the ambiguity over what was produced, the District must label the responses to correspond with the RFP. CCP 2031.280 (in other words, I find for the limited purpose of this RFP that it is insufficient to rely on production as records are kept in the usual course). RFP 33 - denied. The District's offer to search "all responsive emails between employees" for the designated key words appears reasonably calculated to capture relevant and responsive material. RFP 34 - granted. The District said it would produce. If it has completed that process it should say so. Both requests for sanctions are DENIED because the motion is granted in part and denied in part.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge by email sent to sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant's demurrers are overruled. Defendant relies on its notion that the Tort Claims Act requires particularized pleading here. However, this is a Fair Employment and Housing Act case and thus exempt from rules that generally apply to claims against government. (Snipes v. City of Bakersfield (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 861, 868.) Accepting, as we must, the complaint's allegations as true, the second, third, sixth and seventh causes of action all adequately plead FEHA claims.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:

CCP 430.41 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. Counsel's declaration does not indicate that the statute was complied with, so the demurrer is ordered off calendar. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the complaint is now due by March 19, 2021.
